item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The 'Relevant Documentation' is what ruins this for me; it reads like the headcanon someone would form if they tried to misinterpret the rest of the article as much as possible. I also can't buy that an average, anomalous creature enthusiast would know about pattern screamers.

Everything else is pretty good (especially for a pattern screamer SCP!), but not enough to effect my distaste for that first Addendum. -1

This was outstanding. I think if it had stopped at Addendum 1 it would've fit in Series 1 with the very best it had to offer. The second addendum adds some character but felt a bit overly formulaic. Not enough to keep me from an enthusiastic +1 though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lovely. This should be tagged with pattern screamer no?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to ask if this is based around the myths of various ghosts, spirits and other creatures found in various cultures around the world that are said to attach themselves to the backs of people and cause illness and bad luck?

I was aware of these kinds of myths, and it definitely fits into that kind of archetype, but I didn't have a specific one in mind when writing.

I did find a potential typo, though. Is "Domesday Clock" supposed to be "Doomsday Clock"?

This is intentional. I may or may not end putting it in article, but the Domesday Clock here is just an anomaly that was around back in those days.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Due to this relatively short length, I was worried that this would just amount to a thing that kills you. I was pleasantly surprised when it was not.

The dialogue is effective, and well constructed, though I don't know what more there is to learn from someone who has been affected by an anomaly for 50 years. Regardless, it was engaging, especially the latter portion with the description of the initial mission, which I found evocative, and the last line, which packs a good punch, though I did expect Snow to actually turn around.

Also, as a lover of mythology, I have to ask if this is based around the myths of various ghosts, spirits and other creatures found in various cultures around the world that are said to attach themselves to the backs of people and cause illness and bad luck?

I did find a potential typo, though. Is "Domesday Clock" supposed to be "Doomsday Clock"?

A definite +1 regardless.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: They come up in quite a few different articles, there's a tag for them here!

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: The pattern screamers are the memetic shark-affecting dolphins, right?

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the vote! The "cousins" referenced in the article are the more openly hostile varieties of Pattern Screamers that show up on the wiki, since the Passengers are the same kind of beings.

This is a nice, short, and well-done article, which is why I'm giving this a hearty +1! However, I did want to ask if the mentioned "cousins" refer to a specific skip, or something you were planning on expanding.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Thanks to etoile, Niblic and Justice for their kind feedback!
